meta.title: RCB vs GT · Final
meta.subtitle: IPL 2026 · Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad · 7:30 PM
meta.disclaimer: Generated before match start · May not reflect live events

blocks.content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru take on 2022 winners Gujarat Titans in the grand finale. RCB is chasing back-to-back titles, while GT looks to capitalize on their formidable home advantage.
Both teams finished the league stage with 9 wins and 18 points, but RCB took the top spot on superior net run rate.
RCB enter with immense confidence after brushing GT aside in Qualifier 1, whereas GT bounced back strongly against RR in Qualifier 2.
RCB: V. Kohli, F. du Plessis, R. Patidar (c), G. Maxwell, C. Green, D. Karthik (wk), M. Lomror, K. Sharma, M. Siraj, Y. Dayal, L. Ferguson.
GT: S. Gill (c), S. Sudharsan, S. Khan, D. Miller, R. Tewatia, R. Khan, N. Ahmad, M. Sharma, J. Little, U. Yadav, W. Saha (wk).
Both teams are expected to field unchanged sides with no fresh injuries reported.
GT's spin duo will look to control the middle overs, while RCB relies heavily on early powerplay strikes.
Kohli's ability to navigate Rashid's four overs in the middle phase could dictate the tempo of RCB's innings.

Narendra Modi Stadium generally offers a good batting surface, but the pressure of a final often makes chasing tricky. RCB has great memories here after lifting their maiden trophy at this venue in 2025.
Shubman Gill has been in blistering touch for GT, scoring 43 off 18 in a recent chase against RCB at this very venue.
